message:
feat(index-worker): implement MVP CLI for single-file content extraction

This commit transforms index-worker from a placeholder into a minimal viable
CLI tool that extracts text from a single file using the content-extractor
stack, enabling end-to-end testing of the extraction pipeline before full
batch job orchestration is implemented.

Changes:
- Update index-worker/Cargo.toml:
  * Add tracing-subscriber for structured logging
  * Add clap with derive feature for CLI argument parsing
  * Add dotenvy for .env configuration loading
- Rewrite index-worker/src/main.rs:
  * Replace placeholder with full CLI implementation
  * Define Args struct with clap Parser derive:
    - --volume-id: u16 for DocKey construction
    - --file-id: u64 for DocKey construction
    - --path: PathBuf to file to extract
    - --max-bytes: usize (default 10 MiB = 10485760)
    - --max-chars: usize (default 100k = 100000)
    - --enable-extractous: bool (default false)
  * Initialize tracing from RUST_LOG env var
  * Load .env via dotenvy for config alignment with service
  * Support ULTRASEARCH_ENABLE_EXTRACTOUS env override for Extractous toggle
  * Build ExtractContext from CLI args (path, extension hint, size limits)
  * Verify file exists before extraction to catch missing files early
  * Build ExtractorStack with optional Extractous backend
  * Run extraction and log bytes_processed, truncated flag, detected language
  * Print 200-char preview of extracted text to stdout
  * Return error on extraction failure for testability

Architecture:
This MVP implementation enables:
1. Manual testing: `cargo run -p index-worker -- --path test.pdf --volume-id 1 --file-id 42`
2. Automated testing: spawn worker process, check exit code and stdout
3. Feature validation: compare SimpleText vs Extractous backends on real files
4. Size limit validation: verify max_bytes/max_chars enforcement
5. Language detection validation: check chardetng output on multilingual docs

The worker currently operates in single-file mode (no batch contract yet).
This is intentional - it allows validating the extraction stack in isolation
before adding:
- Batch job protocol (accept list of DocKeys + paths from service)
- IPC result reporting (success/failure counts, extracted text upload)
- Retry/backoff logic (handle transient file locks, corrupted documents)
- Resource limits (memory caps, timeout per file)

The CLI matches the service's configuration philosophy:
- CLI flags for per-invocation settings (path, limits)
- Env vars for deployment-wide policy (ULTRASEARCH_ENABLE_EXTRACTOUS)
- .env file for local dev overrides

The enable_extractous flag integration matches the content-extractor API:
- False (default): ExtractorStack::with_defaults() uses SimpleText + Noop
- True: ExtractorStack::with_extractous_enabled(true) adds Extractous extractor
- Aligns with extractous_backend Cargo feature flag (c00.5.2)

Example usage:
```bash
# Extract with SimpleText backend (fast, limited format support)
cargo run -p index-worker -- \
  --volume-id 1 --file-id 100 \
  --path /mnt/c/Users/test/document.txt

# Extract with Extractous backend (slower, handles PDF/Office formats)
ULTRASEARCH_ENABLE_EXTRACTOUS=1 cargo run -p index-worker -- \
  --volume-id 1 --file-id 200 \
  --path /mnt/c/Users/test/report.pdf \
  --max-bytes 52428800 \
  --max-chars 500000
```

Next steps (not in this commit):
- Define batch job contract (c00.5.1): accept Vec<Job> from service
- Add IPC result reporting (c00.5.1): upload extracted text to content-index
- Integrate with scheduler spawn policy (c00.4.4): service spawns worker on DeepIdle
- Add worker process priority setting (c00.7.4): IDLE/BELOW_NORMAL class
- Add timeout enforcement (c00.5.1): kill worker if stuck on corrupted file

Related: c00.5.2 (Extractous feature flag), c00.5.1 (worker job contract),
         c00.4.4 (scheduler spawn policy), c00.7.4 (process priorities)
